item.title: Why, and to what extent, may a false hypothesis yield the truth?
Deaths by Covid Vaccination Status - England
2019 Multidimensional Poverty Index - Going beyond averages
One Day of Citibike Rides
item.description: A visualization of every Citibike trip taken on March 6th, 2014.

item.title: Enigma Datathon - Analyzing NYC Subway Data
item.description: Recently, I teamed up with a few fellow grad students at NYU to compete in a datathon sponsored by Enigma, who provided several datasets related to public transit in NYC and instructed teams to use them for analysis in a very open-ended way. Our team decided to look at three different metrics - subway ridership, traffic, and subway performance - and see if we could find any interesting relationships between them. The code and results of our analysis are on available on GitHub.

item.title: For a quick pass at fuzzy string matching, consider TF-IDF vectorization
item.description: While competing in the Enigma Datathon at NYU, my team and I were analyzing a dataset of Metrocard usage at New York City subway stations. Our goal was to obtain the precise location of each station, and ultimately do some spatial analysis of the relationship between ridership and other spatial data, like traffic conditions around the city. We found a database of station names and coordinates, but there was no ‘station ID’ field that could be used to match stations from the two datasets. Our only option was to use the station names, and here’s what the names from each dataset looked like:

item.title: Mapping Every Type of 311 Call in New York City
Asteroids Scratch Demo
item.description: An unfinished remake of the classic arcade game I created as part of my work with WHEDco.

Greetings and thank you for visiting my personal website.

I’m a Data Platform Engineer with nearly a decade of experience building scalable cloud systems, with a focus on containerization, orchestration, and data pipelines that emphasize horizontal scalability and reproducibility. I hold a B.S. in Mechanical Engineering from Johns Hopkins University (2016) and an M.S. in Data Science from New York University (2021).

In my spare time (and often at work), I love exploring datasets and creating interactive visualizations that make complex information more accessible and engaging. This site features some of my favorite side projects in this space, including geospatial analyses and real-time data explorations.
